context.py

1#!/usr/bin/env python3
2import argparse, os, sys, subprocess, textwrap, unicodedata
3from pathlib import Path
4
5DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_DIRS = {
6 ".git","node_modules","dist","build",".venv","venv","__pycache__",
7 ".tox",".mypy_cache",".pytest_cache",".idea",".vscode",".DS_Store"
8}
9DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_EXT = {
10 ".png",".jpg",".jpeg",".gif",".webp",".svg",".pdf",".zip",".tar",".gz",".bz2",".7z",
11 ".mp3",".wav",".flac",".mp4",".mov",".avi",".mkv",
12 ".exe",".dll",".so",".dylib",".bin",".class",".o",".a",".jar",
13 ".otf",".ttf",".woff",".woff2",".ico",".lock"
14}
15DEFAULT_SECRET_BASENAMES = {
16 ".env","id_rsa","id_ed25519","credentials.json","service-account.json","secret.json",
17 "secrets.json","firebase-service-account.json","google-credentials.json"
18}
19DEFAULT_PRIORITY_FILES = [
20 "README.md","README","README.rst","README.txt",
21 "pyproject.toml","requirements.txt","environment.yml","Pipfile","Pipfile.lock",
22 "package.json","yarn.lock","pnpm-lock.yaml","go.mod","go.sum",
23 "Cargo.toml","Cargo.lock","Gemfile","pom.xml","Makefile","Dockerfile",".env.example"
24]
25
26def is_binary(path: Path, sample_bytes=8192) -> bool:
27 try:
28 with path.open("rb") as f:
29 chunk = f.read(sample_bytes)
30 if b"\x00" in chunk:
31 return True
32 # Heuristic: too many non-text characters
33 text_ratio = sum(32 <= b <= 126 or b in (9,10,13) for b in chunk) / max(1, len(chunk))
34 return text_ratio < 0.80
35 except Exception:
36 return True
37
38def git_ls_files(repo: Path):
39 try:
40 out = subprocess.check_output(["git","ls-files"], cwd=repo, text=True)
41 return [repo / p for p in out.splitlines()]
42 except Exception:
43 # Fallback to walk
44 return [p for p in repo.rglob("*") if p.is_file()]
45
46def within_size_limits(path: Path, max_bytes_per_file: int) -> bool:
47 try:
48 return path.stat().st_size <= max_bytes_per_file
49 except Exception:
50 return False
51
52def looks_texty(path: Path) -> bool:
53 # Allow files without extensions if not binary
54 ext = path.suffix.lower()
55 if ext in DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_EXT:
56 return False
57 return True
58
59def normalize(s: str) -> str:
60 s = unicodedata.normalize("NFKC", s)
61 # Strip trailing spaces to save tokens
62 return "\n".join(line.rstrip() for line in s.splitlines())
63
64def read_head(path: Path, max_lines: int) -> str:
65 try:
66 with path.open("r", encoding="utf-8", errors="replace") as f:
67 lines = []
68 for i, line in enumerate(f, 1):
69 lines.append(line)
70 if i >= max_lines:
71 break
72 return normalize("".join(lines))
73 except Exception as e:
74 return f"[Could not read file: {e}]"
75
76def main():
77 ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Build a compact context.md from a repo.")
78 ap.add_argument("--repo", default=".", help="Path to repo root")
79 ap.add_argument("--out", default="context.md", help="Output file")
80 ap.add_argument("--max-lines-per-file", type=int, default=400)
81 ap.add_argument("--max-bytes-per-file", type=int, default=200_000)
82 ap.add_argument("--max-total-bytes", type=int, default=5_000_000)
83 ap.add_argument("--depth", type=int, default=3, help="Tree depth")
84 args = ap.parse_args()
85
86 root = Path(args.repo).resolve()
87 files = git_ls_files(root)
88
89 # Filter
90 candidates = []
91 for p in files:
92 rel = p.relative_to(root)
93 parts = set(rel.parts)
94 if any(d in parts for d in DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_DIRS):
95 continue
96 if p.name in DEFAULT_SECRET_BASENAMES:
97 continue
98 if not looks_texty(p):
99 continue
100 if is_binary(p):
101 continue
102 if not within_size_limits(p, args.max_bytes_per_file):
103 continue
104 candidates.append(p)
105
106 # Put priority files first
107 priority = []
108 others = []
109 priority_names = set(DEFAULT_PRIORITY_FILES)
110 for p in candidates:
111 if p.name in priority_names or str(p.relative_to(root)) in DEFAULT_PRIORITY_FILES:
112 priority.append(p)
113 else:
114 others.append(p)
115 ordered = priority + sorted(others, key=lambda x: str(x).lower())
116
117 # Build header
118 def make_tree(root: Path, depth: int) -> str:
119 lines = []
120 def walk(base: Path, level: int):
121 if level > depth:
122 return
123 entries = sorted([p for p in base.iterdir() if p.name not in DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_DIRS], key=lambda x: (x.is_file(), x.name.lower()))
124 for e in entries:
125 indent = " " * (level - 1)
126 lines.append(f"{indent}- {e.name}{'/' if e.is_dir() else ''}")
127 if e.is_dir():
128 walk(e, level + 1)
129 lines.append(f"{root.name}/")
130 walk(root, 1)
131 return "\n".join(lines)
132
133 header = [
134 "# Repository context for LLM",
135 "",
136 "## How this was built",
137 "- Files are text only and truncated to a head limit.",
138 "- Common binary and build artifacts are excluded.",
139 "- Likely secret files are excluded.",
140 "",
141 "## Directory tree (truncated)",
142 "```",
143 make_tree(root, args.depth),
144 "```",
145 ""
146 ]
147
148 total_bytes = sum(len(s)+1 for s in header)
149 chunks = ["\n".join(header)]
150
151 for p in ordered:
152 if ("/tests/" in str(p)) or ("/CAMBFiles/" in str(p)) or (".example." in str(p)) or ("HMF_Validation" in str(p)) or ("example/log" in str(p)):
153 continue
154 rel = str(p.relative_to(root))
155 body = read_head(p, args.max_lines_per_file)
156 block = f"\n\n----- FILE: {rel} -----\n```text\n{body}\n```"
157 b = len(block.encode("utf-8"))
158 if total_bytes + b > args.max_total_bytes:
159 chunks.append("\n\n[Truncated due to max_total_bytes limit]\n")
160 break
161 chunks.append(block)
162 total_bytes += b
163
164 out = "\n".join(chunks)
165 Path(args.out).write_text(out, encoding="utf-8")
166 print(f"Wrote {args.out} ({total_bytes} bytes)")
167
168if __name__ == "__main__":
169 main()
max
#define max(a, b)
